full_text: MRI is good in studies it comes with sort of a relatively equal comparison to transvaginal scan the benefit of transvaginal scan is it's cheaper and it's more accessible however it does depend on the skill of the person using it like with everything okay so even more so than MRI MRI is about the interpretation of the result transvaginal scan is about how you use the probe and then interpret the results that you get MRI is very effective in some hospitals it's what's available you know it that's people's practice and they will tend to have radiologists who are extremely efficient and expert analyzing MRI result MRI is great for assessing endometriosis affecting above the rectal sigmoid so that's up to the large bowel the cecum the appendix small bowel going upwards it can be very effective in the pouch of Douglas however in my experience I find that sometimes there is not a correlation between the laparoscopic findings and the MRI findings often the laparoscopic findings which is the gold standard is more indicates worse thing so that's one of the issues with MRI is sometimes it doesn't correlate with laparoscopic findings and when you're doing your procedure sometimes not always but sometimes you may have to do a two stage procedure because it hasn't given you enough information and in fact you do need to do either further investigations or further procedure with a colorectal surgeon
